strategies.name: Task Category to Copilot Assignment Correlation
strategies.finding: Security/functional tasks get agents; lib version bumps do not

strategies.name: Historical Trend Regression Analysis
strategies.finding: Duration >15min = 100% success; <5min = ~7% success. Agent count >=4/day = 48.8% success

strategies.name: Temporal Analysis
strategies.finding: Duration improving 12min/day. Duration strongest predictor of session health

strategies.name: CI Smoke Test Failure Clustering
strategies.finding: Simultaneous failures at same timestamp = infra incident not code regression. 3+ failures same second = infra rule

strategies.name: Gate Workflow Composition Fingerprinting
strategies.finding: Gate count = complexity proxy: 1 gate = trivial, 4-7 = moderate, 9+ = feature addition. Security Review Agent co-occurrence = high-risk signal. Haiku Printer only = auto-merge candidate.

strategies.name: Gate Sweep Branch Concentration
strategies.finding: When 1 branch dominates >40% of daily sessions (here 23/50=46%), it is a routine/templated workflow (community contributions update). Branch dominance correlates with low task complexity and tight duration variance.

strategies.name: Gate Sweep Burst Sizing
strategies.finding: Burst-size distribution per branch reveals iteration shape: one large burst (14 fires at single timestamp) + many small bursts (3-4) = single push triggering full gate-set followed by drip retries. Investigate-otel-connection branch fits this profile: 14-burst at 07:12:15Z = new commit, 5 smaller bursts at 06:39-06:43 = polling/cleanup. Largest single burst size correlates with code-change scope; small-burst frequency correlates with reviewer/auto-retry activity.

strategies.name: Inverse Gate-Count to Conclusiveness
strategies.finding: Per-branch gate-firing count is strongly inversely correlated with the rate of conclusive (success/failure) outcomes within the sampling window. On 2026-05-21: 3-session branch (refactor-oversized-functions-parser-workflow) = 100% conclusive (2 success + 1 failure); 5-session branch = 20%; 7-session = 14.3%; 10-session = 10%; 25-session (fix-duplicate-regex-patterns) = 4%. Hypothesis: branches with low gate count have either narrow scope (gates run once and resolve) or recent agent intervention (sweep already cleared); branches with high gate count are caught in a retry loop. Predictive use: branches above ~15 gates in a 1-hour window are likely waiting on agent action, not on green CI. Combine with Gate Sweep Branch Concentration (low diversity day) and Gate Workflow Composition Fingerprinting (gate-set complexity proxy) for a 3-axis branch-health classifier.
